fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space; Perimeter Fencing
fr.recommendations: To support effective emergency response outcomes, ensuring unobstructed access routes to the property and around the main building is crucial. Addressing the dense vegetation could enhance navigation and visibility for emergency responders, providing clear lines of sight and easier movement in and around the property. Establishing clear rear access to the property would support emergency responder ingress and egress, ensuring quicker response times in situations requiring entry from multiple points. Additionally, adapting the layout to ensure that all key areas, such as entrances and pathways, are clearly visible and accessible may facilitate prompt and effective response efforts. Maintaining open green spaces and ensuring perimeter fencing does not impede access points could assist in creating efficient staging areas for emergency personnel and vehicles, enhancing overall operational efficiency.

wildfire.conditions: Dense Tree Canopy; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Tree Cluster; Defensible Space; Vegetation Between Structures
wildfire.recommendations: Improving defensible space around the property could be achieved by managing the dense tree canopy and reducing fuel continuity, particularly where trees are in close proximity to structures. Increasing the separation between scattered trees and structures can also support access for firefighters and help limit fire spread. Particular attention to vegetation adjacent to structures should be focused on removal or adjustment to reduce exposure. Maintaining or expanding open terrain areas and ensuring the vegetated perimeter does not create a continuous fuel bed can further enhance defensible space. It may be beneficial to manage vegetation between structures to reduce the potential for fire transfer. Preserving clear areas around the tree cluster will help isolate potential fire sources and maintain open space to reduce fuel load. Continued attention to vegetation management at the property perimeter could support long-term wildfire resilience and help maintain effective defensible space.
